faqs.question: How can I tell if I have carpenter ants versus regular ants?
faqs.answer: Carpenter ants are significantly larger than common household ants, typically 1/4 to 1/2 inch long, and are usually black or dark brown. You may notice piles of wood shavings (frass) near wooden structures, hear faint rustling sounds inside walls, or see large winged ants in spring. Unlike termites, carpenter ants don't eat wood but excavate it to build nests.

faqs.question: Why are carpenter ants so common in Lindenhurst?
faqs.answer: Lindenhurst's proximity to waterways and high water table creates moisture issues in many homes, which attracts carpenter ants seeking damp or decaying wood. The area's mature trees and wooded lots also provide outdoor colonies that eventually migrate into homes. Older construction along the canal areas is especially vulnerable to moisture damage and carpenter ant infiltration.

faqs.question: How quickly can you eliminate a carpenter ant infestation?
faqs.answer: Most carpenter ant treatments show significant results within 7-10 days, with complete elimination typically achieved within 2-4 weeks. The timeline depends on colony size and location. We use targeted baiting systems that worker ants carry back to the colony, eliminating the queen and entire nest. Follow-up visits are scheduled to ensure complete eradication and prevent re-infestation.
